revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ishiura-jinja]][[ja:石浦神社]] {{Expand Japanese|石浦神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ishiura Shrine | native_name = 石浦神社 | image = Ishikawa Kanazawa Ishiura00.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ishiura Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.561194|136.659833}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|fr|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|mni|ꯑꯣꯃꯣꯅꯣꯅꯨꯁꯤ|ru|Омононуси|simple|Ōmononushi|zh|大物主|qid=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}} | website = {{Official website|https://www.ishiura.jp/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ishiura Shrine'''|石浦神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * Province}} ** Series ordinal: 32 ** P155: {{ill|Nomano Shrine|lt=Nomano Shrine|qid=Q135040289|1=Nomano Shrine (Q135040289)}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135040291|ltq=Q135040291|qid=Q135040291|1=Kamo Shrine (Q135040291)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q11520078|ltq=Q11520078|qid=Q11520078|1=Hondamachi}} * {{ill|Kaga Province|lt=Kaga Province|cs|Provincie Kaga|de|Provinz Kaga|en|Kaga Province|eo|Provinco Kaga|es|Provincia de Kaga|fa|ولایت کاگا|fi|Kagan provinssi|fr|Province de Kaga|gl|Provincia de Kaga|id|Provinsi Kaga|it|Kaga (provincia)|ja|加賀国|ko|가가국|lt|Kagos provincija|nl|Kaga (provincie)|pt|Província de Kaga|ru|Кага (провинция)|simple|Kaga Province|sk|Kaga (provincia)|sr|Провинција Кага|tr|Kaga (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Каґа|zh_min_nan|Kaga-kok|zh_yue|加賀國|zh|加賀國|qid=Q907469|1=Kaga Province}} * {{ill|Q1130942|ltq=Q1130942|qid=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}} == coordinate location (P625) == * Ōjin}}https://www.ishiura.jp/yuisho/index.html == YouTube handle (P11245) == * きまちゃんねる-v6eYouTube API ** P2397: UC0KU2jqY-EqPWr9LgV6LSpQ ** Point in time: {'time': '+2025-11-26T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Facebook username (P2013) == * 199561000078992 == YouTube channel ID (P2397) == * UC0KU2jqY-EqPWr9LgV6LSpQ ** P1810: きまちゃんねる ** P3744: {'amount': '+44', 'unit': '1'} ** P3740: {'amount': '+20', 'unit': '1'} ** P5436: {'amount': '+2776', 'unit': '1'} ** Start time: {'time': '+2018-10-25T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** Point in time: {'time': '+2020-12-17T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 9608431 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3220005000945 == Commons category (P373) == * Ishiura-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Miwa Shrine|lt=Miwa Shrine|qid=Q135040290|1=Miwa Shrine (Q135040290)}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '石川県金沢市本多町三丁目1番30号', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '石川県河北郡津幡町北中条', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Torii of Ishiura Shrine from Mayumizaka Entrance of Kenroku Garden.JPG {{commons category|Category:Ishiura-jinja}} [[JA:石浦神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11586806}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ishiura Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Ishikawa Kanazawa Ishiura00.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|qid=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|qid=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}{{ill|lt=Honda-machi (Kanazawa)|Q11520078|qid=Q11520078|lt=Honda-machi|1=Hondamachi}} 3-1-30 |祭神 = Ōmononushi Ōkami |社格 = Shikinaisha small-shrine candidate / former prefectural shrine |創建 = Traditionally during Tenpyō period (729-748) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Yamato Hase Kannon, Hasezan Jikōin, Ishiurazan Jikōin Hase-dera, Ishiura Sannō, Jinushi Gongen, Ishiura Daigongen |札所等 = Former second site of old Japan's three great Hase-dera |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|qid=Q2932|1=October 2}} |神事 = |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 33 |緯度秒 = 40.3 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 39 |経度秒 = 35.4 |ラベル位置 = |地図 = Japan Kanazawa city2#Japan Kanazawa city }} '''Ishiura Shrine''' (いしうらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Honda-machi (Kanazawa)|Q11520078|qid=Q11520078|lt=Honda-machi|1=Hondamachi}}, {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|qid=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|qid=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was prefectural shrine. At foundation it was called "Miw a Shrine"[https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/247613 New monument at Ishiura Shrine; 100 years since grandfather's donation] {{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|qid=Q904678|1=Chunichi Shimbun}}, viewed 2021-05-06.. It is a candidate for the small shrine "Miwa Shrine" in Kaga District, {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q907469|qid=Q907469|1=Kaga Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and is also said to be the oldest shrine in Kanazawa. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi Ōkami|1=Ōmononushi}} * {{ill|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui Ōkami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kikurihime Ōkami|1=Kikurihime}} (also called Shirayama-hime Ōkami) * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumeramikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane Ōkami}} (also called Kasuga Ōkami) * {{ill|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime Ōkami|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|qid=Q261637|lt=Honda-wake Ōkami|1=Hachiman}} (also called Hachiman Ōkami) === Origin === Traditions include: clans of Miwa in {{ill|lt=Kaga District (Echizen)|Q1131574|qid=Q1131574|1=Kaga District, Okayama}} built a shrine in the Nara period and invited Ōmononushi from {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|qid=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} as "Miwa Shrine"; or it was founded by imperial order in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 11 ({{ill|lt=739|Q23588|qid=Q23588|1=739}}); or by order of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kazan|Q361000|qid=Q361000|1=Emperor Kazan}}; or it began as Hase Kannon Hall in Ishiura, founded in Yōrō years (717-723) by a woman of the Matsuura clan inviting Hase Kannon of Yamato's Hase-dera. Although various theories exist, it was revered as the sōsha of seven villages of Ishiura-gō in Kaga District and considered the shikinaisha listed as "Miwa Shrine" among 13 seats of Kaga District under the Engi systemAnother candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Tsubata)|Q11357406|qid=Q11357406|lt=Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine (Q11357406)}} in Tsubata, Kahoku District.. With the rise of honji-suijaku-based shinbutsu shūgō, because the invited Ōmononushi was considered identical with the deity of {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|qid=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Shrine|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|qid=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, this shrine came to be called "Ishiura Sannō" or "Jinushi Gongen." Also, because the principal image of Ōmiwa's jingūji {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa-dera|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} was eleven-headed Kannon, this shrine's honji-butsu was also treated as Jūichimen Kannon; it was called "Yamato Hase Kannon," later "Hasezan Jikōin" by late Kan'ei years, and served by bettō temple clerics. The original site (Nara-period foundation) is said to have been in Shimo-Ishiura village (around present Nagamachi 3-ban-chō), with traditions placing it in districts of samurai estates in the Edo period. In the Muromachi period there was a large shrine building there. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|qid=Q6712|1=1580}}), when {{ill|lt=Sakuma Morimasa|Q986345|qid=Q986345|1=Sakuma Morimasa}} attacked Kanazawa Gobō, base of the Kaga Ikkō-ikki, the shrine burned completely. The sacred body and Buddhist image had been jointly enshrined then; the honji Jūichimen Kannon was first placed at Atago-sha in Yamazaki village of Kodatsuno, then moved to Kannon-in on Utatsuyama. In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1601|Q6725|qid=Q6725|1=1601}}), Maeda Toshinaga granted over 600 tsubo at Honda-machi; next year (March 29), shrine buildings were rebuilt at the former Ishiura fort site in Kami-Ishiura village and the shrine returned to Ishiura. In {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|qid=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|qid=Q6784|1=1615}}), the land was incorporated into the lower residence of domain elder {{ill|lt=Honda Masashige|Q11520055|qid=Q11520055|1=Honda Masashige}}. In Kanpō 3 ({{ill|lt=1743|Q7580|qid=Q7580|1=1743}}), another 170 tsubo were added, totaling over 770 tsubo. Records in ''Kokujizasshō'' (letter from Jikōin, 1709) describe enshrined triad correspondences of Sannō Gongen and associated honji-butsu. Parishioner area covered seven Ishiura-gō villages and extended from around Musashigatsuji to the left bank of the Saigawa in present Kanazawa, a broad area. Thus the shrine was revered both as ubusuna of Kanazawa castle town and as castle guardian by successive Kaga lords. Toshinaga rebuilt its halls; under fifth lord Tsunanori, safe childbirth prayers were offered for his wife, strengthening faith in the shrine as a deity of safe delivery. The 50,000-koku Honda family also treated it as a prayer site, making repeated donations and reconstructions. Originally the shrine enshrined Ōmononushi as Miwa Shrine, but the prominence of honji-butsu Jūichimen Kannon and the common name Jikōin led people to recognize it as a temple, and other shrines emerged claiming the Miwa name and shikinaisha status. However, it is argued to be clear this shrine is the Engishiki Miwa Shrine because ''Kaga no Kuni Shikinaisha Kyūshaki'' copied in 1653 records: "Miwa Shrine, one seat in Engishiki, located at Ishiura village of Ishiura-gō, called Ishiura Sannō, sōsha of seven villages of Ishiura-gō, now in Ishikawa District." In the late feudal era, though affectionately called Ishiura Sannō / Jinushi Gongen / Jikōin, after the Meiji shinbutsu separation decree it was renamed "Ishiura Shrine" in Dec 1868 after the parish area name; in Sep 1880 it moved from Shimo-Honda-machi to its current address. === Festivals === * {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|qid=Q2932|1=October 2}} 10:00 - annual festival There are over 50 festivals yearly (major, medium, minor). At the pre-Setsubun festival in {{ill|lt=2020|Q25337|qid=Q25337|1=2020}}, sumo wrestler {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishiura|1=UNKNOWN}} (same surname as shrine name) was invited{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000023048|title = Kanazawa Ishiura Shrine bean-throwing with sumo wrestler Ishiura|publisher = Hokuriku Shinkansen Tourism Navi (Hokkoku Shimbun)|date = 2020-02-03|accessdate = 2020-04-26}}. === Stone monuments === A stone monument reading "Ishiura Shrine" was erected beside the approach in 1921. In 2021, another monument reading the original name "Miwa Shrine" was erected beside it. === 101 Torii === [[File:101Torii.jpg|thumb|101 Torii]] In {{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|qid=Q25274|1=2019}}, Ishiura Shrine built 101 torii on the north side of the precinct{{Cite news|url = https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000022621|title = 101 torii lined up at Ishiura Shrine, new symbol completed|publisher = Hokuriku Shinkansen Tourism Navi (Hokkoku Shimbun)|date = 2019-12-24|accessdate = 2020-04-26}}{{Cite news|url = https://kanazawa.keizai.biz/headline/3347/|title = 101 torii at Ishiura Shrine become new Kanazawa landmark|publisher = Kanazawa Keizai Shimbun|date = 2020-01-14|accessdate = 2020-04-26}}. Nicknamed "101 Torii," they were developed over half a year from July in commemoration of the Reiwa era change and enthronement of Emperor Naruhito. The walking route is about 50 m and is illuminated at night. It has become a topic on social media such as Instagram. === Access === ; Bus * {{ill|lt=Hokutetsu Bus|Q65249557|qid=Q65249557|1=Hokutetsu Bus}}: get off at {{ill|lt=Hirosaka (Kanazawa)|Q17226445|qid=Q17226445|lt=Hirosaka|1=Hirosaka}} / {{ill|lt=21st Century Museum of Contemporary Art, Kanazawa|Q3242206|qid=Q3242206|lt=21st Century Museum|1=21st Century Museum of Contemporary Art, Kanazawa}} * {{ill|lt=Kanazawa Furatto Bus|Q11647354|qid=Q11647354|1=Kanazawa Flat Bus}} (Kikugawa route): get off at 21st Century Museum stop === Nearby information === * {{ill|lt=Kenroku-en|Q998239|qid=Q998239|1=Kenroku-en}} * {{ill|lt=21st Century Museum of Contemporary Art, Kanazawa|Q3242206|qid=Q3242206|1=21st Century Museum of Contemporary Art, Kanazawa}} * {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefectural Government Memorial Shiinoki Guest House|Q11585956|qid=Q11585956|1=Shiinoki Cultural Complex}} * {{ill|lt=Kanazawa Castle|Q511412|qid=Q511412|1=Kanazawa Castle}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ishiura-jinja}} * [https://www.ishiura.jp/ Ishiura Shrine] - official site * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j0251/ Ishiura Shrine] - Ishikawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [https://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/spot/4595 Hot Ishikawa Travel Net: Ishiura Shrine] - Ishikawa Tourism Federation == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ishiura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ishiura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kanazawa, Ishikawa]] [[Category:金沢市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]]
